source_intake_record.raw_question_text:
A simple harmonic transverse wave propagates along the positive direction of the $x$-axis. The equilibrium position of the wave source is at the origin of the coordinate system. The vibration graph of the wave source within $0 \sim 4s$ is shown in the figure. It is known that the wave propagation speed is $0.5\mathsf{m}/\mathsf{s}$.

1. Determine the wavelength of this transverse wave.
2. Determine the distance traveled by the wave source within 4s.

image_dependency: question_text explicitly says the vibration graph is shown in the figure
requires_image=true and text_dominant=false in the input
key quantities for both subquestions (such as period/amplitude from the source graph) are not stated in text and must be read from the image
multi_step: subquestion 1 requires extracting a time-domain property from the graph and combining it with the given wave speed to get wavelength
subquestion 2 requires reading the oscillation magnitude/period from the graph and computing total travel distance over 4 s
the problem has two linked numerical outputs, indicating more than a single direct lookup
verifiability: answer_type is short_text with explicit numeric targets (lambda and distance)
given wave speed is provided and the graph should allow rule-based checking of period/amplitude extraction and downstream calculations
asset_registry_record shows question_present=true, answer_present=true, and registry_passed=true

decision_reasons: 题干、答案和主图像均已存在，核心语义资产完整，满足进入候选池的基本条件。
初步评分较高：图像依赖强、多步推理明显、可验证性较好，具备较高保留价值。
虽然存在“possible_graph_scale_ambiguity”风险，但这属于标注与解析层面的不确定性，不构成核心语义缺失。
